skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
test_name: March 2025 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
passage: A certain elephant calf weighed 226 pounds when it was born. The elephant calf gained an average of 3 pounds per day in its first year of life. Which equation models this situation, where y is the estimated weight of the elephant calf, in pounds, x days after it was born?

skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
test_name: March 2025 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
passage: Audrey's hair will grow at a constant rate from a length of 7 inches to a length of 11 inches. The equation $0.6t+7=11$ describes this situation, where t is the number of months it will take for Audrey's hair to reach a length of 11 inches. Which of the following is the best interpretation of 0.6 in this context?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The time, in months, it will take Audrey's hair to grow 1 inch

choices.label: B
choices.text: The length, in inches, Audrey's hair will grow each month

choices.label: C
choices.text: The time, in months, it will take Audrey's hair to reach a length of 11 inches

choices.label: D
choices.text: The length, in inches, Audrey's hair will grow in 11 months

test_name: March 2025 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
passage: A rectangle has a length that is 31 times its width. The function $y=(31w)(w)$ represents this situation, where y is the area, in square feet, of the rectangle and $y>0$. Which of the following is the best interpretation of 31 w in this context?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The length of the rectangle, in feet

choices.label: B
choices.text: The area of the rectangle, in square feet

choices.label: C
choices.text: The difference between the length and the width of the rectangle, in feet

choices.label: D
choices.text: The width of the rectangle, in feet

How many visitors are located in room C?

passage: The positive number a is 2,136 % of the sum of the positive numbers b and c, and b is 89 % of c. What percent of b is a?
